reasoning: The Lean formalization matches the informal statement cleanly. The informal "arbitrary nonempty collection" (with the explicit instruction not to assume countability) is captured by an arbitrary type `I : Type*` with `[Nonempty I]` as an instance. The family of normal subgroups is `H : I → Subgroup G` together with `hH : ∀ i, Normal (H i)`. The "intersection" corresponds to `⨅ (i : I), H i`, which in Mathlib's Subgroup lattice is literally the set-theoretic intersection (via `Subgroup.coe_iInf`). The conclusion `Normal (⨅ i, H i)` is the standard Mathlib predicate `Subgroup.Normal`. No hypotheses are added or dropped beyond what the informal statement specifies, and the quantifier structure (universal over the family, existence/nonemptiness of the index) is preserved. This is a faithful formalization.
